items.title: Malawi's former finance minister arrested for corruption as post-election crackdown continues
items.description: Malawian police arrested the former finance minister Sunday on suspicion of money laundering and abuse of office, in the latest move in a widening post-election crackdown on former senior officials.

items.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
items.description: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

items.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
items.description: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

items.title: Canadian PM Mark Carney secures majority government in special election
items.description: Voters cast ballots for three vacant seats of the 343 districts represented in Parliament

items.title: Residents near cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel
items.description: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

items.title: Bloom Energy to supply up to 2.8 GW of fuel cells under expanded Oracle deal
Hiring, wage growth likely to ease in 2026 due to Middle East conflict: MAS
items.description: The central bank said the overall business outlook has softened and employment growth is expected to ease from the gains seen in 2025.

items.title: China: Evergrande property developer founder pleads guilty
items.description: China's property developer giant has become its also its most indebted and defaulted on most of its liabilities and payments in 2021. Founder Hui Ka Yan "pleaded guilty and expressed remorse," the court said.

items.title: The warning signs a health practitioner wants to exploit your superannuation
items.description: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

items.title: U.K. will not back blockade of Strait of Hormuz, says PM Starmer
items.description: Britain had minesweepers in the ​region, Starmer said, and ‌while he could not discuss operational matters, the military capability was “focused from our point of view on getting the Strait full open”

items.title: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
items.description: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

items.title: French court jails Lafarge former CEO for funding IS in Syria
items.description: A French court on Monday fined the cement group Lafarge over $1.3 million and sentenced its former boss to six years in prison for paying protection money to the Islamic State group and other jihadists to maintain its business in war-torn Syria. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Korean AI chip startup DeepX prepares public share offering
Qld's child safety department 'hamstrung' by underfunding, inquiry hears
items.description: An inquiry has heard ambitious goals to improve Queensland's child safety department were unrealistic as it struggled to recover from pressures caused by the COVID pandemic.

items.title: Middle East war live: China says US blockade of Iran ports 'dangerous and irresponsible'
items.description: China said on Tuesday that a US blockade around Iranian ports was "dangerous and irresponsible", after US President Donald Trump threatened to sink any boats that sought to leave or dock there. Earlier, the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah urged Lebanon to pull out of direct talks with Israel set to take place in Washington, the first such talks in decades. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: What impact will the Iran war have on the wedding industry?
items.description: The wedding industry in the UK is gauging the impact of the war with Iran. Despite riding fuel prices putting pressure on supply chains, vendors say they are prepared to absorb higher costs.

items.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
items.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

items.title: Spain seeks closer China links amid global tensions
items.description: Pedro Sánchez meets Xi Jinping in Beijing on Tuesday 14 April 2026, aiming to strengthen political and trade relations at a time of global tension.

items.title: Wegovy-maker Novo Nordisk partners with OpenAI to speed drug development
Dieselgate: Audi bosses, engineers set for long trial as plea deal fails
items.description: Two former Audi bosses and two engineers are facing charges over "Dieselgate," a massive emissions scandal that still haunts Germany's automobile sector. As no plea deals could be reached, a long trial seems likely.

items.title: Israel-Iran war highlights: Trump vows to destroy Iranian warships that get near U.S. blockade
items.description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said a ceasefire between the United States and Iran was “holding” and that efforts were under way to reach an agreement

items.title: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation faces investor scrutiny amid strategy shift, FT reports
Pope Leo XIV’s Africa tour sparks hope, faith and calls for peace
items.description: Anticipation is building across Africa as Pope Leo XIV prepares for an 11-day այց spanning four nations, with believers expressing hope for spiritual renewal and political reconciliation.

items.title: U.K. inquiry lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at dance class
items.description: An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.

items.title: UK finds attack on Taylor Swift-themed class 'preventable'
items.description: A new report on the stabbing that left little three girls dead in UK's Southport concluded that the killer's parents and state authorities failed to act on his violent behavior.

items.title: Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall's UN bid
items.description: Burundi nominated former Senegal president Macky Sall for UN chief, but Africa's backing for him has fractured. AU procedural disputes and regional politics have scuppered a united endorsement.

items.title: Benin's opposition candidate concedes defeat to Wadagni in presidential vote
items.description: Benin opposition candidate Paul Hounkpe suffered a bruising election loss on Monday after conceding to favourite Romuald Wadagni in Benin's presidential vote.

items.title: Australia's largest cattle feedlot on the market as grain-fed beef surges
items.description: Mort & Co, which fattens hundreds of thousands of cattle on grain before being slaughtered for beef, puts the majority of its assets on the market.

items.title: Yamal seeks inspiration from LeBron, Neymar for Champions League comeback
items.description: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal sees Neymar as his idol and wants to watch him play in the World Cup for Brazil.

items.title: Australia's Qantas warns of big bill from higher jet fuel prices
items.description: The cost of jet fuel in the second half of 2026 is now expected to be A$3.1 billion to A$3.3 billion, up from A$2.5 billion in Qantas' previous forecast.

items.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
items.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

items.title: Trump uses McDonald’s Oval Office stunt to tout new US tip tax break
items.description: Donald Trump staged a McDonald’s DoorDash delivery to the Oval Office to promote new tax deductions on tips, touting relief of up to $25,000.

items.title: Russia offers to take in Iran's enriched uranium
items.description: Failed talks between Iran and the United States over the weekend dashed hopes of a swift deal to permanently end the war that has killed thousands and thrown the global economy into turmoil since it began in late February

items.title: US drivers head to Native American lands for cheaper gas
items.description: Some of the cheapest fuel in the country can be found on tribal land due to tax exemptions.

items.title: Wadagni claims landslide victory in Benin presidential vote
items.description: Romuald Wadagni has secured a decisive win in Benin’s presidential election, taking 94 percent of the vote according to provisional results released by the electoral commission.

items.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
items.description: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

items.title: Benin leans into painful past to encourage cultural tourism
items.description: Benin is leaning into its painful history as a key port in the transatlantic slave trade to encourage tourism.

items.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku bears down on Northern Mariana Islands, Guam
items.description: Slow-moving Sinlaku is weakening as it heads towards Guam, but it will still bring dangerous conditions to the region.

items.title: Interfaith dialogue: 'The messenger of peace in the name of Pope Leo XIV is coming to visit us'
items.description: As Pope Leo XIV makes a historic visit to Algeria, in the spirit of interfaith dialogue, François Picard is pleased to welcome Father Fred Wekesa, Rector of the Basilica of Saint Augustine in Annaba. From his experience of living over a decade in Algeria, he challenges inherited narratives shaped by the violence of the 1990s, highlighting the present reality of coexistence and tolerance. He argues that interreligious dialogue must extend beyond institutions into families and daily life.

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close
items.description: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

items.title: US stocks gain, dollar dips on hopes for Iran war negotiations
France: Parliament debates bill to simplify return of stolen artworks
items.description: It still has tens of thousands of artworks and artefacts that it looted from its colonial empire. Now, France is debating a bill to simplify returning them to their countries of origin.

items.title: Indonesia, US sign ‘major’ defence cooperation agreement
items.description: Signing of defence partnership follows reports that US is seeking overflight access in Indonesia for military planes.

items.title: Made lot of progress, ball in Iran’s court: Vance
items.description: The U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement in the 21-hour marathon peace talks in Islamabad over the weekend, with Washington insisting that Tehran refused to give up its right over enrichment of nuclear fuel

items.title: PSG travel to Anfield to seal a Champions League semi-final place
items.description: The Parisians head to Anfield for the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool. After a 2–0 first-leg win, the Paris club arrive with a strong advantage and are targeting a place in the Champions League semi-finals.

items.title: Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf neighbours
items.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US ​military blockade of ships leaving Iran's ports, and Tehran threatened to retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down. Oil prices surged, with no sign of a swift reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to ease the biggest ever disruption in supplies. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: BBC joins paramedics on duty in Lebanon after Israeli strikes
items.description: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

items.title: UN urges ‘all parties’ to respect navigation in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: UN Secretary-General is calling on ‘all parties’ to respect freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
items.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.

items.title: Lebanon and Israel to hold rare direct talks in Washington despite Hezbollah opposition
items.description: Lebanese and Israeli envoys meet in Washington on Tuesday for the first face-to-face talks in decades between representatives of the two countries, mediated by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah has warned it will not abide by any agreements that may result from the talks, describing them as "futile".

items.title: US military kills two men in new strike on vessel in eastern Pacific
items.description: Latest attack brings death toll from US strikes on vessels in the Pacific and Caribbean to at least 170 since September.

items.title: Spain PM Pedro Sanchez’s wife charged with corruption after years-long probe
items.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, has been formally charged with corruption following a years-long investigation, a court ruling said on Monday. The probe, opened in 2024, examines whether she used her position for private gain, allegations she and Sanchez deny, increasing pressure on his minority government.

items.title: Benin’s Wadagni wins presidential election with landslide 94% of votes
items.description: The incoming president faces serious security issues in the north of the country and challenges to living standards.

items.title: U.S. lawmaker Swalwell resigns over sexual misconduct allegations
items.description: Eric Swalwell's troubles escalated rapidly over the weekend as reports in the San Francisco Chronicle and CNN detailed allegations from four women

items.title: Coalition's migration proposal faces practical questions
items.description: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

items.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again
items.description: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major change.

items.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in separate Gaza strikes
items.description: Two Palestinians were killed in separate strikes in southern Gaza on Monday, according to officials at Nasser hospital.

items.title: Rescuers free young whale after 24-hour stranding ordeal
items.description: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been freed by marine animal rescue teams.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump says Tehran wants deal amid US blockade in Hormuz
items.description: Iran accuses US of committing piracy as thousands rally in Tehran against the blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Can this man broaden the appeal of a South African party seen by some as 'too white'?
items.description: The Democratic Alliance's new leader, Geordin Hill-Lewis, admits the party's support is too narrow.

items.title: Hungary's Magyar outlines policy in first news conference
items.description: Prospective prime minister Peter Magyar has promised to move Budapest back towards the European Union and away from Russia. However, he is likely to continue Viktor Orban's hardline policies on immigration.

items.title: Trump’s genocidal threat to Iran and an elusive deal
4 policemen injured in militant attack on polio team in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
items.description: The injured were shifted to a nearby hospital for treatment, officials said

items.title: Poland withdraws from treaty banning antipersonnel mines, will use them to defend against Russia
items.description: Poland, which ratified the document in 2012 and completed the destruction of its domestic anti-personnel mine stockpile in 2016, withdrew from the treaty on February 20, 2026 and says it plans to renew manufacturing weapons

items.title: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
items.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

Buenos Aires Book Fair turns 50 with a focus on memory
items.description: Fiftieth anniversary edition will revisit censorship during the dictatorship, honour Jorge Luis Borges and expand into immersive formats about future of reading and publishing in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
items.description: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

items.title: US: Fugitive Brazilian ex-spy chief arrested by ICE
items.description: Former Brazilian intelligence chief Alexandre Ramagem has been detained by ICE in the United States. Brazil formally requested his extradition in December.

items.title: Global markets slide as Donald Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: Global markets have been rattled after Donald Trump announced a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions following failed peace talks with Iran.

items.title: Millions of Rockstar Games business records stolen, hacking group says
Donald Trump 'hasn't read the psychology of Iranians well at all', military expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, former British Military Intelligence officer Philip Ingram says that "Donald Trump hasn't read the psychology of the Iranians well at all" adding that "the Iranians are not going to give in, and therefore Donald Trump either has to continue the blockade and deal with the impact that that's going to have on global economies [...] or go back to a bombing campaign".

items.title: Japan volcano erupts sending plumes of ash 3.4 km high
items.description: The eruption of Sakurajima was the first major eruption since December 13

items.title: Hungary will be "a tough partner, but a partner" on Ukraine, expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Lukáš Macek, Director of the Greater Europe project at the Jacques Delors Centre, says on the question of Ukraine the new Hungarian government led by Peter Magyar is likely to stop obstructing aid for Ukraine and will be "a tough partner, but a partner nonetheless" on the issue of Ukrainian accession to the EU.

items.title: Trump says he won't apologize to Pope Leo after criticism
items.description: President Donald Trump refused to apologize to Pope Leo XIV on Monday after criticizing the pontiff for his opposition to the war in Iran — and he sought to explain away a now-deleted social media post depicting himself as Jesus by saying he had thought the image was of him as a doctor. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called Trump's remarks "unacceptable". FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

items.title: Pollution concerns at remote NT mine as 'rogue' operator stands down dozens of workers
items.description: Nathan River Resources has stood down more than 40 workers since Good Friday. Now financial, environmental and safety issues at the mine are being raised, as the NT government is accused of weak regulation.

items.title: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
items.description: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

items.title: Magyar's landslide victory over Orbán 'showed the limits of right-wing populism in Europe'
items.description: Following the landslide victory of Peter Magyar over Hungary's long standing autocratic ruler Viktor Orbán, Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Ian Bond, former Senior British Diplomat and Deputy Director of the Centre for European Reform. Bond sees this election within broader European political dynamics, particularly the limits of right-wing populism, the legacy of hardline governance, and the implications for EU cohesion and Ukraine policy. Hungary’s political shift invites cautious optimism, explains Bond. Change will be partial and contested rather than immediate or transformative.

items.title: Orban ousted: What Magyar’s victory means for Hungary and the EU
items.description: Peter Magyar's landslide victory over Viktor Orban in Sunday’s Hungarian legislative elections marks a seismic shift for Hungary and the EU, but Brussels may need to temper its enthusiasm. On the rule of law, migration and LGBTQ rights, the road ahead is neither straight nor guaranteed.

items.title: Hungarian voters celebrate 'much-needed' change
items.description: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar.

items.title: 'Slow-moving car crash': ASIC investigating billionaire's empire after company collapses
items.description: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

items.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
items.description: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

items.title: 'Stop hiring humans'? Silicon Valley confronts AI job panic
items.description: The anxiety is well-founded. More and more companies are directly citing AI in announcing job cuts

items.description: Prosecutors allege two men were chauffeured back to NSW after conducting a contract killing of the Melbourne underworld figure in 2023.

items.description: Panel details incidents of violence by Israeli forces and settlers against displaced Palestinians in Gaza and West Bank.

items.description: Halliburton says it has secured a multibillion-dollar contract from Argentina’s state-run oil company YPF to provide bundled fracking services at Vaca Muerta. Leer más

items.description: Port Adelaide's acting captain Zak Butters will face the AFL Tribunal to fight allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?" during the game against St Kilda. Follow live.

items.description: The BBC spoke to people in Sydney to hear their thoughts on the Duke and Duchess of Sussex’s trip to Australia.

items.title: Donald Trump deletes AI image of himself as Jesus after attacking Pope Leo
items.description: Donald Trump launched an extraordinary attack on Pope Leo late Sunday, also going as far as to share an AI-generated image depicting himself as Jesus Christ healing the sick. He later deleted the image Monday following backlash from his usually loyal Christian and Catholic supporters, saying he “thought it was me as a doctor.”

items.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
items.description: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV pushes back as Donald Trump criticism escalates
items.description: US-born Pope Leo XIV pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s broadside against him over the U.S.-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration.

items.title: Airstrike in Nigeria kills dozens, civilians feared among victims
items.description: At least 56 people were killed and 14 injured after a military airstrike hit a market in northeastern Nigeria, according to a UN report, with locals and rights groups warning that civilians were among those killed.

items.title: Two congressmen resign as US House faces rare expulsions over scandals
items.description: Two US lawmakers stepped down on Monday and two others faced possible expulsion amid scandals roiling Congress. Democrat Eric Swalwell resigned after misconduct allegations, while Republican Tony Gonzales said he would retire following pressure over an affair with a former aide, deepening turmoil in the House of Representatives.

items.title: Burundi court partially acquits jailed journalist after two years
items.description: A Burundian court partially acquitted journalist Sandra Muhoza on Monday after nearly two years behind bars.

items.description: The colossal steel plants along Ukraine's side of the sea are unrestorable ruins since Russia's invasion.

items.description: As India marks the 135th birth anniversary of BR Ambedkar, DW's special coverage explores the gap between the promise of Dalit upliftment and the reality today.

items.description: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

items.description: Four men who were allegedly involved in a "sophisticated" operation to steal "hundreds" of crocodile eggs from Kakadu National Park have faced court in Darwin.

items.description: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

items.title: World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends

items.title: Trump deletes image of himself as Jesus as feud with pope dismays US Catholics
items.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday deleted a social media image depicting him as Jesus after an outcry from religious leaders that he was being blasphemous. The outrage added to widespread dismay among US Catholics over Trump’s unprecedented verbal assault on Pope Leo XIV, the first American to lead their church.

items.title: End of an era: Why did Hungary vote out far-right's Orban?
items.description: Eurosceptics may come and go... but there's only one Viktor Orban. It took 16 years and Sunday's record turnout in Hungary to vote out the seemingly unbeatable far-right leader who once boasted of building an illiberal state. Why did the far-right idol of Magaworld finally fall out of favor? And not just by a nose: “The Hungarian people didn’t vote for a simple change of government, but for a complete change in regime,” boasts prime minister-in-waiting Peter Magyar. How will the once-ally-turned-pro-EU conservative turn his constitutional supermajority into a rolling back of Orban's consolidation of power.

items.title: News24 | Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall’s UN bid
items.description: Burundi nominated former Senegal president Macky Sall for UN chief, but Africa’s backing fractured. AU procedural disputes, objections and regional politics scuppered a united endorsement.

items.title: Crans-Montana mayor says unaware of six-year safety lapse before Swiss bar fire that killed 41
items.description: Crans-Montana Mayor Nicolas Feraud said on Monday he was unaware safety checks had not been conducted for six years at a Swiss ski resort bar where a fire during New Year celebrations killed 41 people. Prosecutors are investigating the blaze, with nine people, including Feraud, under criminal investigation over the disaster.

items.title: US maritime restrictions increase economic pressure on Iran
items.description: A US naval blockade will cut off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will Donald Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

items.title: Ukrainian civilians abducted, tortured in Russian prisons
items.description: Russia has been holding thousands of Ukrainian civilians behind bars for years, in breach of international humanitarian law. Families have been fighting for their loved ones to be released, some for more than a decade.

items.title: Benin heads to a pivotal transition as votes are counted
items.description: Benin began counting ballots Sunday after a presidential election that could extend a decade of economic growth while testing the country’s democratic balance. Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the frontrunner to succeed outgoing president Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after two terms.

items.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
items.description: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

items.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
items.description: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

items.title: Environmental NGOs seek ‘largest collective lawsuit in history’ in defence of Argentina’s glaciers
items.description: Environmental groups call on members of the public to join a massive collective lawsuit against President Javier Milei’s changes to Argentina’s Glacier Law, which were approved by the lower house in the early hours of Thursday morning. Leer más

items.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
items.description: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

items.title: News24 | Sentebale launches legal action against Prince Harry over ‘reputational harm’
items.description: Sentebale, a charity co-founded by Prince Harry, is suing him for harming its reputation through a media campaign.

items.title: Hezbollah leader urges Lebanon’s government to pull out of Israel talks
items.description: Naim Qassem says planned talks in Washington, DC, are a ploy to pressure Hezbollah into laying down its weapons.

items.title: MoS Margherita attends mourning ceremony for late Iranian leader Ayatollah Khamenei
items.description: The ceremony was organised by the Iranian embassy on Sunday (April 12, 2026) to pay respect to the departed leader on the 40th day (Chehelum) of his demise

items.title: India, Oman discuss ways to boost trade, investments
items.description: India will reduce tariffs on Omani products such as dates, marbles and petrochemical items, under the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement

items.title: Iran's new Supreme Leader is reportedly recovering from severe wounds
items.description: The news agency Reuters said on Sunday that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was recovering from severe and disfiguring wounds from the airstrike that killed his father, Ali Khamenei. Iran's leader has not been seen in public since his appointment.

items.title: South Korea, Poland to upgrade ties as Donald Tusk calls Seoul key ally after U.S.
items.description: South ⁠Korean President Lee said the countries would further expand defence ⁠industry cooperation under a $44.2 billion framework pact signed in 2022

items.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
items.description: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

items.title: News24 | Record turnout Hungarian election could end Orban’s 16-year rule as pro-EU challenger leads
items.description: Hungarians flocked to vote on Sunday in a closely watched parliamentary election that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year stint in power as a self-described “thorn” in the EU’s side.

items.title: CFMEU breached industrial laws at least 25 times in 14 years, inquiry hears
items.description: Former Australian Building and Contruction Commission head Nigel Hadgkiss has given evidence at the Commission of Inquiry into the CFMEU in Queensland.

items.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
items.description: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein to face retrial on New York rape charge
items.description: Former Hollywood magnate Harvey Weinstein is again going on trial on a rape charge in New York City. He will remain imprisoned for other offences regardless of the verdict.

items.title: Mauritius vows to pursue Chagos sovereignty after UK deal collapses
items.description: Mauritius has pledged to “spare no effort” to reclaim control of the Chagos Islands after a proposed handover deal by Keir Starmer collapsed amid shifting U.S. support.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Thailand marks Songkran New Year with water fights and travel surge
items.description: Thailand marked the start of the Songkran New Year on Monday, 13 April 2026, with large crowds gathering in Bangkok for its traditional water festival.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Leading candidate quits California governor race amid sexual misconduct claims
items.description: Top Democratic allies intensified pressure on him to exit the gubernatorial race.

items.title: News24 | Orban concedes defeat to Magyar after 16-year rule in Hungary
items.description: Nationalist Viktor Orban, who has ruled Hungary for 16 years, on Sunday conceded defeat to conservative Peter Magyar in parliamentary elections.

items.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
items.description: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

items.title: Peru election extended for second day after ballot delivery failure
items.description: Peruvians headed back to the polls Monday after ballot shortages forced an unprecedented extension of Sunday’s election, reflecting deep strains in a country crippled by political instability and escalating crime. Peru police on Monday said they arrested an election official amid an enquiry into the voting delays.

items.title: Trump hosts Dutch royals at the White House for dinner and overnight stay
items.description: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands were received by US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the White House on Monday, 13 April 2026, for a formal dinner and overnight stay as part of their three-day visit to the United States.

items.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 in 2025, more hangings feared: NGOs
items.description: The number of executions represented an increase of 6% on the 975 people Iran put to death in 2024, and also included 48 women, Norway-based Iran Human Rights (IHR) and Paris-based Together Against the Death Penalty (ECPM) said in their joint annual report

items.title: Wanted man found injured, detained by residents, dies in hospital
items.description: A man wanted by NSW Police dies in hospital from injuries sustained after being detained by two men near Murwillumbah.

items.title: U.S., Israel met ‘strategic defeat’ in Iran, says Ambassador Fathali
items.description: The Envoy said the U.S. side made "unlawful" demands during the talks that led to the negotiations ending without results

items.title: Israel says troops fighting Hezbollah surrounded key Lebanon town
items.description: "The forces of the 98th Division have completed the encirclement of the town of Bint Jbeil and have begun an assault on it," the military's Arabic-language spokesman Colonel Avichay Adraee said on X.

items.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
items.description: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Benin's finance minister wins presidential election with 94%
items.description: According to provisional results, Romuald Wadagni, the election favorite, won a landslide victory in Benin's presidential election.

items.title: Pressure on Adorni builds as investigators probe assets, travel, bank details
items.description: President Javier Milei’s Cabinet chief is facing mounting questions over his finances after journalistic investigations uncovered inconsistencies, unexplained asset growth and transactions involving third parties; Judge requests banking secrecy rules to be lifted to aid probe. Leer más

items.title: Iranian crude returns to India after seven years as tankers dock at key ports
items.description: The one-month exemption permitted the sale of Iranian oil already in transit, aimed at easing global supply disruptions and containing prices

items.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
items.description: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guiness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

items.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
items.description: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

items.title: After New York setback, mystery surrounding Burford’s hidden YPF litigation partners grows
items.description: Over and above the forfeited gains of the law office that litigated against Argentina, the big losers of the recent New York Court of Appeals ruling over the nationalisation of YPF are the investors who bought up part of the suit. The story of a trial which still shrouds identities – a mystery only a US judge will be able to elucidate. Leer más

items.title: Pope Leo XIV urges immediate ceasefire as Israel-Hezbollah clashes rage
items.description: Pope Leo XIV called for an immediate ceasefire on Sunday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues, urging all parties to respect international law and protect civilians.

items.title: Argentina to play Honduras and Iceland in pre-World Cup friendlies
items.description: 'La Scaloneta' will face Honduras on June 6 in Texas and Iceland’s senior side on June 9 in Alabama ahead of World Cup kick-off. Leer más

items.title: Cabinet chiefs past and present
items.description: The Cabinet chief’s role as whipping boy can be traced back to the post’s origins in the 1995 constitutional reform. Leer más

items.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
items.description: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

items.title: News24 | Italy's Meloni issues rare criticism of Trump after his attack on pope
items.description: US President Donald Trump’s attack on Pope Leo was “unacceptable,” Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Monday, joining politicians of all colours in springing to the pontiff’s defence.

items.title: Pope Leo pays his respects at Algeria-France war monument
items.description: Pope Leo touched down in Algeria on Monday, beginning an 11 day tour of four African countries. Leo is visiting Algeria to champion Christian-Muslim co-existence.

items.title: Iran war: Tehran slams US port blockade
items.description: Iran has called the US blockade of its ports a "grave violation" of its sovereignty. US President Donald Trump has said Iranian representatives have asked for a deal following fruitless peace talks. DW has more.

items.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
items.description: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

items.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
items.description: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: Bernie Sanders vows to push resolution to block US weapons to Israel
items.description: Bill is unlikely to pass in Republican-controlled Senate, but it could test waning support for Israel among Democrats.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Ukraine, Russia renew drone strikes after Easter truce
items.description: An infrastructure facility was hit in the central-eastern Dnipropetrovsk region, Ukrainian authorities said

items.title: Israeli fire kills three in Gaza following new ceasefire talks
items.description: Medics said the strike had hit a group of men outside a school in Deir al-Balah in the central Gaza Strip. There was no immediate comment by the Israeli military

items.title: Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Koch says
items.description: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

items.title: China dismisses Vice Minister of Foreign Affairs Sun Weidong
items.description: Vice Foreign Minister Sun Weidong's sudden dismissal comes amid a wave of removals amid anticorruption campaign.

items.title: Bending Spoons cuts Eventbrite staff, rolls out product changes after takeover
News24 | Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens retaliation against Gulf states
items.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US military blockade of ships leaving Iran’s ports, and Tehran threatened to ‌retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down.

items.title: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
items.description: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

items.title: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
items.description: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

items.title: Three babies born into Sudan war every minute, charity warns
items.description: Sudanese children are being born into conditions 'no child should ever face', Save the Children says.
